site.title: NRL TipBot Briefing 2026
site.subhead: Round 9 live board for tipping, charity betting, and weekly NRL intel
site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner bet tracking, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 9 preview notes, charity betting discipline, and weekly NRL intel.

ticker: cowboys comeback win killed the bulldogs charity play early in round 9
the dolphins beat melbourne 26-10 and the storm have now lost seven straight
the mark hughes foundation ledger slips to 2 and 2 and back to minus $3.50 after the $10 dogs miss
broncos, knights and panthers now carry extra weight as the remaining key live reads
do not chase the loss live just because the round started ugly
the right job now is steady tracking not emotional recovery betting

featurePanels.title: Lock Pick
items: The Cowboys comeback win ripped up the clean-read theory fast
That drags the charity ledger back to 2-2 and -$3.50 overall
The only smart move now is not to chase it

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: Storm have now lost seven straight and are fully in the no-trust bucket
The Bulldogs are still too error-prone to be treated as a safety blanket
Broncos and Knights remain the high-leverage reads for the rest of the weekend
A bad Friday can tempt revenge betting if the process is not kept calm
Do not let the 8 from 8 round get mentally cancelled out by one ugly night

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Track whether the Broncos actually convert all those returning stars into a win
See if the Knights really look different with Ponga, Best and Marzhew back
Keep an eye on Panthers versus Manly with Turbo still sidelined
Treat the remaining round as a tipping challenge, not a betting rescue mission
Stay calm enough to use the bad result as information rather than a trigger

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Whether Brisbane are really back or just attracting hope money
How sharp Newcastle look with the cavalry returning
Whether Penrith keep treating Turbo-less Manly like target practice
Any signal that the next charity bet should go smaller and cleaner

betCard.title: Round 9 Charity Bet
why: Canterbury still look more settled top to bottom and should control the game better.
North Queensland lose Murray Taulagi and have to reshuffle again out wide.
At $1.77 on the TAB board, the number looked playable enough to take the shot.
risks: The Bulldogs are without Viliame Kikau and Harry Hayes, so they are not at full strength either.
Tom Dearden and Drinkwater can still drag the Cowboys into chaos-ball contests.
The Bulldogs were still too error-prone and the Cowboys punished it.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 won and collected $10.00.
Round 7: Wests Tigers head to head at $1.53 lost in a 21-20 defeat to Brisbane.
Round 8: No charity bet placed, bank held at $6.50.
Round 9: Bulldogs head to head at $1.77 lost after a Cowboys comeback win.

watchlist: Whether the Bulldogs are still playable once the market settles.
How much the Broncos and Knights improve with key names back on deck.
Any value opening in Panthers-Manly if people over-fade Turbo's absence.
Which Round 8 reads still carry forward once the new team lists hit.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 9 has opened with a punch to the nose
lines: As of Friday, 1 May 2026, the Bulldogs charity play has already missed and the Storm have stretched their skid to seven straight losses.
That takes the Mark Hughes Foundation ledger to 2-2 and back to -$3.50 after the larger $10 Bulldogs stake.
The challenge now is staying rational through the rest of the round instead of trying to win the pain back in one move.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: The Bulldogs charity read missed and the lesson matters
lines: The Bulldogs still looked like the cleaner pre-game read, but their sloppiness stayed in the game long enough for North Queensland to come back and punish it.
That is the hard part of betting discipline: a reasonable number and a decent process can still lose cleanly.
Storm also falling over again removes one of the earlier market-against ideas from the trust bucket entirely.
The important thing is to log the miss honestly and not let it infect the remaining tipping calls.

sections.title: Round 9 Fixtures

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who deserves the Round 9 upgrade or downgrade
lines: Hot: Bulldogs, Panthers, Sharks, the full-strength Knights.
Cold: Eels, the Turbo-less Sea Eagles attack, and any blind faith in last week's market lines.
The biggest swing teams are Broncos and Knights because their named line-ups are so much stronger.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The data points most worth checking before locking Round 9
lines: The biggest injury and availability pivots are Turbo still out, Murray Taulagi out, and the Broncos stars all returning together.
Kalyn Ponga, Bradman Best and Greg Marzhew back for Newcastle make Knights-Rabbitohs one of the trickiest reads on the slate.
Luke Metcalf on the Warriors bench and the unchanged Eels squad make Parramatta-New Zealand more competitive than the ladder suggests.
Treat the next charity bet like a fresh decision, not a carry-over from the 8 from 8 tipping high.

footerNotes: Built from the attached Claude prototype and reorganised into a maintainable Codex workflow.
Use this as a weekly operating board, then swap in live-verified NRL data when you want to publish or bet.
